text:
Anthropic is now valued at nearly a trillion dollars.

Not a typo.

The company behind Claude just filed for an IPO at a $965 billion valuation — the most valuable private AI company on earth.

When that much capital concentrates around AI, one thing becomes clear: the people who understand these tools — who build with them, who shape how they're used — are not on the sidelines of history.

You don't have to found an AI lab to matter in this era.

But you do have to show up. Learn the tools. Build something. Understand what you're working with and why it matters.

AI fluency isn't a nice-to-have. It's the new literacy.

The future belongs to builders.

visual.openai_prompt: Create a cinematic Layer8Culture visual in a deep black environment with electric blue accent lighting, premium creator-tech atmosphere, late-night builder energy, documentary realism, human-centered AI themes, Black tech futurism, minimal composition, strong negative space, warm practical lighting, and high contrast. The scene is a built-out premium broadcast studio (not a makeshift setup): a blank neon sign panel on the wall (an unlit, lettering-free glowing panel — do NOT render any text or letters; the wordmark is composited in afterward), the standalone 8 motif on a hoodie or mug, a branded mic flag, a stream deck on a dark wood desk, and a bookshelf washed in blue accent lighting. A confident Black creator/host is present and engaged — direct-to-camera or actively presenting, never anonymous side profiles. The image should feel like a premium technology documentary and creative studio brand. Scene: a confident Black creator/host stands at the center of a premium broadcast studio, one hand gesturing toward a vast wall of abstract electric-blue interconnected network nodes — spheres and lines of glowing light suggesting the scale of global AI infrastructure, no text or numbers anywhere, deep black environment, the standalone 8 motif visible on his hoodie, warm walnut desk in the foreground with a stream deck, cinematic wide composition with generous negative space above, dramatic contrast between the dark studio and the blue data-node glow. Avoid generic startup office, bright corporate lighting, cheap gamer RGB, cluttered composition, cartoon props, excessive sci-fi armor, robots, fake hologram overload, random neon colors, copyrighted franchise references, garbled screen text, random figurines, tripod-mounted monitors, makeshift setups, rendered text, signage lettering, and garbled typography.

text:
Here's what just changed in AI workflows:

Skills.

Not prompts. Not models. Skill files — reusable instruction sets that tell an AI agent exactly how to behave in a specific context.

We're running them inside this content engine right now.

One skill researches the news.
Another generates the posts.
Another enforces the brand voice.
The human approves.

That's not automation. That's a system.

The shift from "ask the AI" to "deploy a skilled agent" is one of the most important changes happening right now — and most people haven't noticed yet.

This is how builders stay ahead.

Comment "skills" if you want to see how we built ours.

text:
Apple made it official at WWDC.

Claude is now an assistant option on iPhone.

You can choose your AI the way you choose your music app, your browser, your notes app — something personal, something that fits how you think.

This moment matters.

Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini — they're not the same. Different strengths, different philosophies, different tradeoffs. Knowing the difference is the new digital literacy.

AI fluency includes knowing your tools. Not just using them — actually understanding what you're working with and why.

So tell us:

Which AI is your daily driver right now, and why?

text:
We ran a 24-hour build session on Tech Thursday.

And something shifts when you lock in that long.

Cam said it after hour 20, tired but still shipping:

"Literally anything you can think of can be built."

Not as hype. As a realization — sitting in the room, watching ideas turn into real things in real time.

The terminal felt less scary. The builds got faster. The momentum fed itself.

Using AI the right way doesn't just help you ship faster. It changes how you see what's possible.

That's what Tech Thursday is. Not a class. Not a recap. The room where the build is actually happening — and you're in it.

Full recap on YouTube. Link in bio.
